theorem: modus_ponens (P Q : Prop) (hpq : P → Q) (hp : P) : Q

steps.label: Apply the implication to its argument
steps.explanation: hpq is a function from P to Q. hp is a proof of P. Applying hpq to hp produces a proof of Q. This is modus ponens — the most basic rule of logic.

strategy.rule: To prove Q when you have P → Q and P, apply the implication
strategy.trigger: You have h : P → Q and a proof of P
